[Full-page diagram description from Gemini: This image is the cover page of a user manual for a technical product. **Overall Purpose**
The image serves as the cover for the "User Guide" for a device called the "iCube II," manufactured by the company Phonak. **Main Subject**
The central focus is a photograph of the iCube II device. It is a small, dark grey or black, rectangular electronic unit with rounded edges. A black cable is attached to the top of the device, forming a large loop that ends in a connector. The device features several interface elements:
* **Top Surface:** The text "iCube II" is printed in small, white lettering. * **Front Face:** There are two small white icons (one resembling a microphone and another indicating connectivity) and a small indicator light. * **Side:** A single button is visible on the side of the unit. * **Bottom Edge:** A small port, likely for charging or data transfer, is located on the bottom. **Text and Annotations**
* **Top Left:** The product name "**iCube II**" is written in a large, dark, serif font. Directly beneath it, the words "**User Guide**" appear in a smaller, lighter font. * **Bottom Right:** The manufacturer's logo, "**PHONAK**," is displayed in a bold, dark, sans-serif font, with the slogan "**life is on**" printed in a smaller, lighter font underneath. * **Bottom Left:** A blue circular icon depicts a white silhouette of a person reading a book, which is a standard symbol for a manual or guide. **Numerical Values**
The only numerical value present is the Roman numeral "**II**" in the product name, indicating this is the second version of the device.]

content:
[Image: This is a product photograph of a black electronic device set against a plain white background. **Main Component**
The central object is a rectangular device with rounded edges and a matte black finish. On the top surface, the text "**iCube II**" is printed in a light gray font. Below this text, there are two headphone icons—one on the left and one on the right—separated by a series of small, horizontal slits that likely function as speaker grilles or indicators. On the right-hand side of the device, there is a side button or marking featuring two opposing arrows (`<>`). At the bottom edge, a small port (likely for charging, such as USB-C) is visible. **Attached Components**
A black cable or strap is attached to the top end of the main body. The cable is secured by two reinforced, ribbed strain-relief sleeves. The cable loops upward and is joined at the top by a thick, segmented black piece, which suggests a connection point or a tensioning mechanism for a wearable strap. **Overall Purpose**
Based on the "iCube II" branding and the headphone icons on the top surface, the device appears to be a portable audio device, a specialized piece of assistive listening equipment, or a sensor-based electronic tool designed for personal use.]
[Image: This is a studio product photograph of an electronic device called the "iCube II," set against a plain white background. **Main Device**
The central component is a matte black, rectangular device with heavily rounded corners. On its top surface, the text "**iCube II**" is printed in white. Below this text, there are two small white headphone icons positioned on either side of two thin, horizontal dashes. On the right side of the device, a button is visible, marked with two white chevron symbols ($>$ and $<$). On the bottom front edge, there is a silver-rimmed USB-C port and a small, circular opening or button to its left. **Cables and Connectivity**
Two black cables emerge from the top of the main unit. These cables loop upwards and are joined together by a small, elongated, pill-shaped black component, creating a neckband-style configuration. The cables feature ribbed, reinforced segments where they meet the main unit and the central connector.

figures.description:
This is a product photograph of a black electronic device set against a plain white background.

**Main Component**
The central object is a rectangular device with rounded edges and a matte black finish. On the top surface, the text "**iCube II**" is printed in a light gray font. Below this text, there are two headphone icons—one on the left and one on the right—separated by a series of small, horizontal slits that likely function as speaker grilles or indicators. On the right-hand side of the device, there is a side button or marking featuring two opposing arrows (`<>`). At the bottom edge, a small port (likely for charging, such as USB-C) is visible.

**Attached Components**
A black cable or strap is attached to the top end of the main body. The cable is secured by two reinforced, ribbed strain-relief sleeves. The cable loops upward and is joined at the top by a thick, segmented black piece, which suggests a connection point or a tensioning mechanism for a wearable strap.

**Overall Purpose**
Based on the "iCube II" branding and the headphone icons on the top surface, the device appears to be a portable audio device, a specialized piece of assistive listening equipment, or a sensor-based electronic tool designed for personal use.

This is a studio product photograph of an electronic device called the "iCube II," set against a plain white background.

**Main Device**
The central component is a matte black, rectangular device with heavily rounded corners. On its top surface, the text "**iCube II**" is printed in white. Below this text, there are two small white headphone icons positioned on either side of two thin, horizontal dashes. 

On the right side of the device, a button is visible, marked with two white chevron symbols ($>$ and $<$). On the bottom front edge, there is a silver-rimmed USB-C port and a small, circular opening or button to its left.

**Cables and Connectivity**
Two black cables emerge from the top of the main unit. These cables loop upwards and are joined together by a small, elongated, pill-shaped black component, creating a neckband-style configuration. The cables feature ribbed, reinforced segments where they meet the main unit and the central connector.

**Overall Purpose**
The design, featuring headphone icons and a neckband-style cable, suggests that this is a portable audio device, likely serving as a wireless/wired audio interface, a transmitter, or a specialized controller for audio equipment.

[Full-page diagram description from Gemini: This image is a page from a technical manual, titled "**2. Description**," which illustrates the components and purpose of the **iCube II** device. **Overall Purpose**
The text states that the intended use of the device is the "wireless transfer of fitting data from the PC to the client's hearing instruments."

    2. "Click the tail of the adapter in to lock it into place."
    * This is illustrated by a diagram on the right. A straight green arrow labeled "**1.**" shows the adapter being pushed horizontally into the main power supply unit. A curved green arrow labeled "**2.**" shows the bottom end (the "tail") being pivoted downward to lock it. * **Removal Instructions:** The bottom section provides a two-step process for detaching the adapter:
    1. "To remove, pull back the tab on the power supply."
    2. "Pull the adapter upwards to remove it."
    * This is illustrated by a second diagram on the right. A downward-pointing green arrow labeled "**1.**" indicates pressing/pulling a small tab on the power supply. An upward/outward green arrow labeled "**2.**" shows the adapter being pulled away from the unit. This image is a page from a technical user manual, specifically page 9, containing informational notes regarding the "iCube II" device. The layout consists of several text blocks set against light gray rectangular backgrounds, each preceded by an information icon (a lowercase "i" inside a circle).

**Text and Annotations:**

*   **Top Note:** An initial informational box states, "iCube II desktop stand is available as an option."
*   **Section Heading:** A bold heading reads, "**Battery notes**."
*   **Battery Instruction 1:** "When charging iCube II for the first time, leave it to charge for at least three hours (even if the middle indicator light changes to green color earlier)."
*   **Battery Instruction 2:** "Charging a depleted battery will take approx. 2 hours. It is safe to leave iCube II connected for longer periods, even if not in use."
*   **Battery Instruction 3:** "A new battery's full performance is achieved only after three complete charge and discharge cycles."
*   **Battery Instruction 4:** "The iCube II battery can be charged several hundred times. If the operating time of your iCube II reduces substantially, please contact your local Phonak representative."
*   **Page Number:** The number "9" is located in the bottom right corner.

**Numerical Values and Specifications:**

*   **Initial Charge Time:** At least 3 hours.
*   **Depleted Battery Charge Time:** Approximately 2 hours.
*   **Performance Threshold:** 3 complete charge and discharge cycles are required for full performance.

**Text and Annotations:**

*   **Top Note:** An initial informational box states, "iCube II desktop stand is available as an option."

*   **Battery Instruction 2:** "Charging a depleted battery will take approx. 2 hours. It is safe to leave iCube II connected for longer periods, even if not in use."
*   **Battery Instruction 3:** "A new battery's full performance is achieved only after three complete charge and discharge cycles."
*   **Battery Instruction 4:** "The iCube II battery can be charged several hundred times. If the operating time of your iCube II reduces substantially, please contact your local Phonak representative."
*   **Page Number:** The number "9" is located in the bottom right corner.

**Numerical Values and Specifications:**

*   **Initial Charge Time:** At least 3 hours.
*   **Depleted Battery Charge Time:** Approximately 2 hours.
*   **Performance Threshold:** 3 complete charge and discharge cycles are required for full performance.
*   **Battery Lifespan:** The battery is rated for "several hundred" charge cycles.

The purpose of this document is to provide essential maintenance, charging, and troubleshooting information for the iCube II battery. It aims to ensure the user achieves optimal battery performance and longevity while providing contact instructions for technical support through Phonak.

This image is a page from a technical manual, specifically section **"5.2 Connecting via USB cable."** The purpose of the page is to provide instructions on how to connect an **iCube II** device to a computer for use with software.

**Text and Labels:**
*   **Heading:** "5.2 Connecting via USB cable"
*   **Instructional Text:** "To connect iCube II via USB cable, insert the mini-USB plug into iCube II and the other end into a free USB port on the fitting PC. Phonak Target 4.0 or newer will automatically detect iCube II and it can be selected as the fitting device."
*   **Information Note:** A gray box at the bottom contains an information icon (a lowercase "i" in a circle) and the text: "Use the optional USB hub if there are no free USB ports on your computer."
*   **Page Number:** "13" is printed in the bottom right corner.

**Illustration and Relationships:**
To the right of the text, a line-art diagram illustrates the connection process. It shows a hand holding a **mini-USB plug**, with a green arrow pointing upward to indicate the direction of insertion into a port on the bottom of a device (the iCube II). The specific port on the device is highlighted with a green rectangle to clearly identify the connection point.

**Specifications Mentioned:**
*   **Device Name:** iCube II
*   **Plug Type:** mini-USB
*   **Software Requirement:** Phonak Target 4.0 or newer

This image is a page from a technical user manual, identified by the heading **"6. Using iCube II."** The page provides instructional text and diagrams for preparing and wearing an iCube II device, specifically focusing on the attachment and use of a neckloop.

**Textual Content:**
*   **Introductory instruction:** It advises users to ensure the battery is sufficiently charged, noting that if the "middle indicator light is orange," the device needs charging. It also states that the iCube II must be selected and shown as the fitting device in "Phonak Target" software.
*   **Section 6.1 "Wearing iCube II":** This section contains three specific steps:
    1.  "Make sure both ends of the neckloop are firmly plugged into the iCube II."
    2.  "Open the neckloop by unplugging the connector in the middle of the neckloop and place iCube II around your client's neck."
    3.  "Close the neckloop to allow the built-in antenna to operate correctly."

**Diagrams and Relationships:**
The text is supported by two line-art diagrams that visually demonstrate the physical actions described:
*   **Top Diagram:** Illustrates the first instruction. It shows the top part of a device with two green-tipped neckloop connectors being inserted into it. Two downward-pointing green arrows indicate the motion required to plug the ends into the device.
*   **Bottom Diagram:** Illustrates the second instruction. It shows a loop-shaped neckloop that is open at a central connector. Two green arrows point away from each other from the center, visually representing the act of unplugging the middle connector to open the loop.

**Specifications and Metadata:**
*   The page number **"14"** is located in the bottom left corner.
*   No specific technical measurements or units are provided, other than the color specification for the indicator light ("orange").

**Overall Purpose:**
The purpose of this page is to provide a clear, step-by-step guide for a technician or user to correctly set up the iCube II hardware, ensuring the neckloop is properly connected and the loop is closed to allow the built-in antenna to function.

section: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
chunk_title: 5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
content:
iCube II is shipped with a pre-paired wireless
USB adapter to ensure a quick and easy
set-up of the wireless connection with your
fitting PC.
iCube II may also be connected with any Bluetooth
capable PC if a USB socket is not available at all or via a
USB cable if a wireless connection is not possible.
5.1 Connecting wirelessly
Automatic connection
To connect iCube II with the fitting PC make sure
that Phonak Target 4.0 and newer is installed. Plug the
USB adapter into a free USB port on your fitting PC.
Make sure the USB adapter is plugged in a USB
port on the fitting PC. Do not use a USB extension
cable to connect it.
--- Page 12 ---
5. Connecting iCube II to your computer
Switch iCube II on and the connection will be
established automatically as soon as Phonak Target is
started.
The USB adapter is pre-paired with iCube II . After
replacement of either the adapter or iCube II ,
a manual pairing is required as described below.
Manual pairing
Switch iCube II on and press the pairing button
for 2 seconds to start the pairing mode.
When in pairing mode, all indicator lights will rapidly
blink back and forth. In Phonak Target, go to Settings ,
select Fitting device and check Enable iCube II .
Go to iCube II via USB adapter and start the Pairing
assistant . It will guide you through the pairing process.
A manual pairing is also required after a reset to
factory settings.
--- Page 13 ---

Before using iCube II , make sure the battery is
sufficiently charged. If the middle indicator light is
orange, please charge iCube II . Make sure that iCube II is selected and shown as the
fitting device in Phonak Target. 6.1 Wearing iCube II
Make sure both ends of the
neckloop are firmly plugged
into the iCube II . Open the neckloop by
unplugging the connector
in the middle of the
neckloop and place
iCube II around your
client’s neck. Close the neckloop to allow the built-in antenna to
operate correctly. --- Page 15 ---
6.2	Detecting and programming hearing instruments
For a wireless fitting, the assignment of the left/right
hearing aid is done in Phonak Target. Make sure that
iCube II is selected as fitting device and click Connect . The detected devices will be shown to assign (or reassign)
each hearing instrument to the desired side (left or right). Click Continue . Follow the instructions on the screen to finish the
detection. --- Page 16 ---
[Full-page diagram description from Gemini: This image is page 16 of a technical manual, specifically under the section "**6. Using iCube II**" and subsection "**6.3 Programming hearing instruments on your desk**."

The purpose of the page is to instruct a user on how to prepare hearing instruments for programming when they are not being worn by a client. The instructional text reads: "If you need to program hearing instuments [sic] outside of the client's ears (e.g. to prepare the hearing instuments before a fitting session), place the hearing instruments on a flat surface and put the neckloop of iCube II around them as shown in the picture."

The page contains two black-and-white line-art illustrations:

*   **Circular Inset:** Located in the upper right, a drawing enclosed in a green circle shows two hearing instruments positioned inside a wide loop. The loop, representing the neckloop of the iCube II, is shown at the top with a connector/plug. This illustration visually demonstrates the instruction to place the hearing aids within the loop. *   **Main Device Illustration:** Below the circle is a drawing of the iCube II device. It is a handheld, rectangular unit with rounded corners. The label "**iCube II**" is printed on the upper half of the device.

This image is page 16 of a technical manual, specifically under the section "**6. Using iCube II**" and subsection "**6.3 Programming hearing instruments on your desk**."

The purpose of the page is to instruct a user on how to prepare hearing instruments for programming when they are not being worn by a client. The instructional text reads: "If you need to program hearing instuments [sic] outside of the client's ears (e.g. to prepare the hearing instuments before a fitting session), place the hearing instruments on a flat surface and put the neckloop of iCube II around them as shown in the picture."

The page contains two black-and-white line-art illustrations:

*   **Circular Inset:** Located in the upper right, a drawing enclosed in a green circle shows two hearing instruments positioned inside a wide loop. The loop, representing the neckloop of the iCube II, is shown at the top with a connector/plug. This illustration visually demonstrates the instruction to place the hearing aids within the loop.
*   **Main Device Illustration:** Below the circle is a drawing of the iCube II device. It is a handheld, rectangular unit with rounded corners. The label "**iCube II**" is printed on the upper half of the device. Two wires extend from the top of the unit. Near the bottom, there are small markings, including two small shield-shaped icons.

The relationship between the components is explained through the text: the iCube II device (shown at the bottom) uses a neckloop (shown in the circle) to surround the hearing instruments for desk-based programming.

This image is a page from a technical manual, titled "**7. Indicator lights**," which explains how to interpret the status of the "**iCube II**" device based on its three indicator lights. The page uses a structured layout with descriptive text on the left, illustrative diagrams in the center, and the corresponding device status on the right.

An introductory sentence states: "There are 3 indicator lights on iCube II for an easy indication of the battery, connection and fitting status."

The page details four specific scenarios regarding the behavior of the **middle indicator light**:

1.  **Charging:** The text on the left says, "Middle indicator remains solid orange." The center diagram shows an orange dash in the middle slot.
2.  **Charging complete:** The text on the left says, "Middle indicator remains solid green." The center diagram shows a green dash in the middle slot.
3.  **iCube II starting up:** The text on the left says, "Middle indicator is solid green for 2 s." The center diagram includes the notation "**2 sec**" above a green dash in the middle slot.
4.  **iCube II switching off:** The text on the left says, "Middle indicator slowly blinking 3 times." The center diagram includes the notation "**3 x**" above the middle slot, which is illustrated with concentric green circles to represent blinking.

Each central diagram depicts a rounded rectangular panel containing three horizontal indicator slots. In every scenario, only the middle slot is active. Below the indicator slots, two small key-shaped icons are visible in each diagram.

The overall purpose of the illustration is to provide a quick-reference guide for users to understand the operational state of the iCube II by observing the color and pattern of its middle indicator light. The page number "**17**" is located at the bottom right corner.

6.2	Detecting and programming hearing instruments
For a wireless fitting, the assignment of the left/right
hearing aid is done in Phonak Target. Make sure that
iCube II is selected as fitting device and click Connect .
The detected devices will be shown to assign (or reassign)
each hearing instrument to the desired side (left or right).
Click Continue .
Follow the instructions on the screen to finish the
detection.

The following pages contain important safety
information. Hazard warnings
Phonak devices may generate magnetic fields. Tests
that Phonak conducted showed that no interfe­
rences with the implantable devices were caused
that would impact the safe and effective use of the
implantable devices. Users of implantable devices
should contact their doctor and/or the manufacturer
of the implantable device before they start using a
Phonak device. Stop using the Phonak device if any
influence of the Phonak device on the implantable
device is experienced and contact the manufacturer
of the implantable device for advice. Keep this device out of reach of children under
3 years. Changes or modifications to the device that were
not explicitly approved by Phonak are not permitted. Phonak is not responsible for situations resulting
from a modified device. --- Page 33 ---
Opening the device might damage it. If problems
occur which cannot be resolved by following the
remedy guidelines in the troubleshooting section
of this user guide, consult your local Phonak
representative. This product has an embedded, non-replaceable
battery. Do not attempt to open the product or remove
the battery as this may cause injury and damage the
device. Dispose of electrical components in accordance with
your local regulations by Phonak AG. Only use accessories approved by Phonak AG, to
avoid any electrical shock. Using the device, its connectors or cables in any way
contradictory to their intended purpose as described
in this user guide (e.g. wearing the USB cable around
the neck) can cause injury. When operating machinery, ensure that no parts get
caught in the machine. --- Page 34 ---
12. Important safety information
The mini-USB port is to be used for the described
purpose only. For safety reasons, only use chargers supplied by
Phonak or stabilized chargers with a rating of 5VDC,
min. 500 mA. Caution: electric shock. Do not insert plug alone into
electrical outlets. Do not use the device in explosive areas (mines or
industrial areas with danger of explosions, oxygen
rich environments or areas where flammable
anesthetics are handled) or where electronic
equipment is prohibited.

Information on product safety
Protect the device from excessive moisture (bathing,
swimming), heat (radiator, car dashboard) or direct
skin contact when sweating. Do not drop the device. Dropping it onto a hard
surface can damage the device. Do not disconnect the neckloop while the device is
transmitting signals to the hearing aids. Protect the device connectors, plugs, charging slot
and power supply from dirt and debris. Clean the device using a damp cloth. Never use
household cleaning products (washing powder, soap,
etc.) or alcohol to clean the device. Never use a
microwave or other heating devices to dry the device. --- Page 36 ---
12. Important safety information
The digitally-coded, inductive transmission
technology used in the device is highly reliable and
experiences virtually no interference from other
devices. It should be noted, however, that when
operating the hearing system near computer
equipment, larger electronic installation or other
strong electromagnetic fields, it may be necessary to
be at least 60 cm (24”) away from the interfering
device to ensure proper operation. Keep the device of at least 10 cm away from any
kind of magnets. Do not connect an USB cable exceeding 3 meters
(9 feet) in length to the device. When the device is not in use, turn it off, keep the
neckloop closed and store it safely. --- Page 37 ---
Other important information
If you use several devices in the same building,
be aware that their inductive field can disturb one
another if within a range of 2 meters (6 feet). This may even occur when used in different rooms. High-powered electronic equipment, larger electronic
installations and metallic structures may impair and
significantly reduce the operating range. If the hearing aids do not respond to the device
because of an unusual field disturbance, move away
from the disturbing field. --- Page 38 ---

Only use accessories approved by Phonak AG, to
avoid any electrical shock.
Using the device, its connectors or cables in any way
contradictory to their intended purpose as described
in this user guide (e.g. wearing the USB cable around
the neck) can cause injury.
When operating machinery, ensure that no parts get
caught in the machine.
--- Page 34 ---
12. Important safety information
The mini-USB port is to be used for the described
purpose only.
For safety reasons, only use chargers supplied by
Phonak or stabilized chargers with a rating of 5VDC,
min. 500 mA.
Caution: electric shock. Do not insert plug alone into
electrical outlets.
Do not use the device in explosive areas (mines or
industrial areas with danger of explosions, oxygen
rich environments or areas where flammable
anesthetics are handled) or where electronic
equipment is prohibited.
--- Page 35 ---
Information on product safety
Protect the device from excessive moisture (bathing,
swimming), heat (radiator, car dashboard) or direct
skin contact when sweating.
Do not drop the device. Dropping it onto a hard
surface can damage the device.
Do not disconnect the neckloop while the device is
transmitting signals to the hearing aids.
Protect the device connectors, plugs, charging slot
and power supply from dirt and debris.
Clean the device using a damp cloth. Never use
household cleaning products (washing powder, soap,
etc.) or alcohol to clean the device. Never use a
microwave or other heating devices to dry the device.
--- Page 36 ---
12. Important safety information
The digitally-coded, inductive transmission
technology used in the device is highly reliable and
experiences virtually no interference from other
devices. It should be noted, however, that when
operating the hearing system near computer
equipment, larger electronic installation or other
strong electromagnetic fields, it may be necessary to
be at least 60 cm (24”) away from the interfering
device to ensure proper operation.
Keep the device of at least 10 cm away from any
kind of magnets.
Do not connect an USB cable exceeding 3 meters
(9 feet) in length to the device.
When the device is not in use, turn it off, keep the
neckloop closed and store it safely.
--- Page 37 ---
Other important information
If you use several devices in the same building,
be aware that their inductive field can disturb one
another if within a range of 2 meters (6 feet).
This may even occur when used in different rooms.
High-powered electronic equipment, larger electronic
installations and metallic structures may impair and
significantly reduce the operating range.
If the hearing aids do not respond to the device
because of an unusual field disturbance, move away
from the disturbing field.
--- Page 38 ---
